text: Which of the following describes the 'Standard' scaling policy for multi-cluster warehouses?

options.a: It favors credit conservation by delaying cluster startup.
options.b: It favors performance by starting clusters immediately when queries are queued.
options.c: It only allows one cluster to run at a time.
options.d: It resizes the warehouse T-shirt size automatically.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows you to share a 'Secure View' with a consumer account, even if that view references data from multiple databases in the provider account.
What is the primary function of 'Micro-partitions' in Snowflake's architecture?

options.a: To allow users to manually partition large tables.
options.b: To store data in an optimized, compressed, columnar format that enables automatic pruning.
options.c: To manage the metadata of the Cloud Services layer.
options.d: To provide a backup of the storage layer.

text: Which role is recommended for daily administrative tasks such as creating warehouses and managing schemas?
What is the maximum Time Travel retention period for a 'Permanent' table in the Business Critical edition?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpipe' uses serverless compute resources provided by Snowflake, not user-managed virtual warehouses.
Which of the following are valid sources for an 'External Stage'? (Choose three.)
What happens when a virtual warehouse reaches its specified 'Auto-suspend' timeout period?

options.a: It resizes to a smaller T-shirt size.
options.b: It is automatically suspended and stops consuming credits.
options.c: It remains active but queries are queued.
options.d: The billing rate is reduced by 50%.

text: Which command is used to see a list of all 'Pipes' in your Snowflake account?
True or False: Data shared via 'Direct Sharing' can be updated by the consumer account.
What is the purpose of 'Time Travel' in Snowflake?

options.a: To speed up historical analytical queries.
options.b: To access and restore data that was modified or deleted within a defined period.
options.c: To archive data to long-term storage.

text: Which layer of the Snowflake architecture handles 'Metadata Management' and 'Security'?
What is the default T-shirt size for a new Virtual Warehouse?
True or False: Snowflake automatically encrypts all data 'at rest' using AES-256.
Which command is used to upload files from a local machine to a Snowflake internal stage?
What does 'Zero-copy cloning' mean?

options.a: Metadata is duplicated but physical data remains shared until modified.
options.b: A physical copy of the data is made instantly.
options.c: No credits are consumed during the cloning process.
options.d: The clone only contains the table schema, not the data.

text: Which view is used to monitor the 'Credit Usage' of a specific virtual warehouse?
True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the individual User level.
What is the primary function of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.b: To restrict row visibility in a table based on user role and session context.
options.c: To manage the number of rows per micro-partition.

text: Which Snowflake edition is the minimum requirement for 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'?
True or False: 'Temporary' tables in Snowflake are automatically dropped at the end of the user session.
Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization Service'?

options.a: It speeds up point lookup queries on large tables by using a background service.
options.b: It is a free tool in the Standard edition.
options.c: It is used for searching files in internal stages.
options.d: It replaces the need for clustering keys.

text: What is the primary benefit of the 'Snowflake Data Marketplace'?

options.a: Organization can discover and access third-party data directly without the need for ETL.
options.b: It provides a place to sell Snowflake hardware.
options.c: Users can buy Snowflake credits at a discount.
options.d: It is where users download Snowflake drivers.

text: Which command is used to see a list of 'Users' in your Snowflake account?
True or False: You can apply a 'Network Policy' to an individual user to override account-level settings.
What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.a: Deleting old data to save storage costs.
options.b: Skipping micro-partitions during a query based on metadata and query filters.
options.c: Reducing the size of a virtual warehouse.
options.d: Cleaning metadata from the cloud services layer.

text: Which role is required to manage 'Organizational' tasks like creating multiple Snowflake accounts?
True or False: Snowflake handles the automatic 'Rotation of encryption keys' for its users.
Which Snowflake component provides the results for a query if it was run within the last 24 hours and data hasn't changed?
What happens to credit consumption when a virtual warehouse is 'Suspended'?

options.d: It is charged a flat monthly fee.

text: True or False: A 'Transient' table persists across sessions but has no Fail-safe period.
Which command is used to download data from a Snowflake stage to a local machine?
What is the primary benefit of 'External Tables'?

options.a: They provide faster query performance than standard tables.
options.b: They allow you to query data sitting in cloud storage without loading it into Snowflake.
options.c: They do not require a virtual warehouse to query.
options.d: They have 90 days of Time Travel by default.

text: Which Snowflake edition is the first to include 'Column-level Security' like Dynamic Data Masking?
True or False: You can apply a 'Masking Policy' to a column in a Snowflake 'View'.
What does the 'ON_ERROR = CONTINUE' parameter do in a COPY command?

options.a: It stops the load at the first error.
options.b: It skips files with errors and continues loading valid data from other files.

text: Which role is primarily used for creating 'Warehouses' and 'Databases'?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Multi-factor Authentication' (MFA) for the classic web interface.
What is the purpose of 'Time Travel'?

options.b: To access and recover data that was modified or deleted within a defined window.
options.c: To archive data to long-term storage.

text: Which Snowflake component handles 'Transaction Management' and 'Metadata'?
What happens when a multi-cluster warehouse is set to 'Auto-scale' and load increases?

options.a: It increases the T-shirt size of the cluster.
options.b: It automatically starts additional clusters of the same size to handle concurrency.
options.c: It moves queries to the cloud services layer.
options.d: It notifies the admin to manually add clusters.

text: True or False: Data shared via a 'Share' object is read-only for the consumer.
Which command is used to see a list of 'Stages' in a schema?
What is the maximum Time Travel retention for a 'Permanent' table in Standard edition?
True or False: Snowflake 'Stored Procedures' can be written in Javascript.
Which describes the 'Economy' scaling policy for multi-cluster warehouses?

options.a: Starts clusters immediately to prevent queuing.
options.b: Only starts a new cluster if it estimates there is enough load to keep it busy for 6 minutes.
options.d: Uses only one cluster regardless of load.

text: True or False: You can apply a 'Network Policy' to an individual user.
What is the primary function of 'Resource Monitors'?

options.b: To limit and monitor the consumption of credits by virtual warehouses.

text: Which Snowflake edition is required for 'PrivateLink' and HIPAA compliance?
True or False: Data shared via 'Private Sharing' is encrypted in transit.
Which Snowflake component handles 'Query Parsing' and 'Optimization'?
What happens if a warehouse size is changed while queries are running?

options.b: Existing queries finish on original resources, new queries use new resources.
options.d: All queries move to new resources immediately.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows 'Object Tagging' for tracking costs and sensitive data.
What is the function of a 'Stream' in Snowflake?

options.a: To load data continuously into a stage.
options.b: To track Change Data Capture (CDC) information for a table.
options.c: To connect to streaming services like Kafka.

text: Which Snowflake interface is a CLI tool?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpipe' uses serverless compute.
What is the primary benefit of 'Clustering' a large table?

options.b: Improves query performance by enabling better data pruning.

text: Which role is the most privileged in Snowflake?
True or False: A 'Permanent' table has a 7-day Fail-safe period in all editions.
Which Snowflake component provides results if a query was run in the last 24 hours with unchanged data?
What happens if a warehouse reaches its credit limit via a Resource Monitor?

options.b: Notifies or suspends the warehouse immediately or after queries finish.

text: True or False: 'External Tables' can query data in Amazon S3.
What is the purpose of 'Dynamic Data Masking'?

options.b: Obscuring sensitive data in results based on user role without changing underlying data.

text: Which Snowflake component handles 'Metadata' and 'Transaction Management'?
True or False: Snowflake handles automatic 'Encryption of data at rest'.
Which command sees a list of 'Resource Monitors'?
What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.b: Skipping micro-partitions without relevant data based on metadata.

text: True or False: A 'Transient' table has a Fail-safe period of 1 day.
Which edition is required for 'Tri-Secret Secure'?
True or False: A 'Permanent' table can be undropped after Time Travel expires.
Which command sees the 'DDL' for a Snowflake object?
What happens if you run 'CREATE OR REPLACE' on an existing table?

options.b: Drops existing, creates new empty table.

text: True or False: 'External Functions' can call APIs on Google Cloud Functions.
Which role manages 'Users' and 'Roles'?
What is the result of 'Scaling Up' a warehouse?
Which describes the difference between 'Standard' and 'Economy' scaling policies? (Choose two.)

options.b: Economy favors credits (starts if load is estimated high enough).

text: True or False: You can share a 'Secure View' joining multiple databases.
What is the primary function of 'Micro-partitions'?

options.b: Columnar, compressed storage enabling automatic pruning.

text: Which role is recommended for daily admin tasks like schemas and data loading?
What is the max Time Travel for 'Permanent' tables in Business Critical?
True or False: 'Snowpipe' uses serverless compute.
Which can be an 'External Stage' source? (Choose three.)
What happens if 'Auto-suspend' is enabled and warehouse is idle?
Which command sees a list of 'Pipes'?
True or False: 'Direct Sharing' is only within the same region.
What is the primary benefit of 'Time Travel'?

options.b: Data recovery/access within a retention period.

text: Which layer handles metadata and security?
What is the default T-shirt size?
True or False: Snowflake handles 'Encryption at rest' using AES-256.
Which command uploads a local file?
What does 'Zero-copy cloning' mean?
Which view monitors credit usage per warehouse?
True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the User level.
What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?
Which edition is required for 'Multi-cluster Warehouses'?
True or False: 'Temporary' tables drop when session ends.
Which describes 'Search Optimization'?

options.a: Speeds point lookups on large tables.

text: What is the benefit of the 'Snowflake Marketplace'?
Which command sees the list of 'Users'?
True or False: Network policies can apply to users.
